steps.title: Use the prior uncertainty
steps.content: The passage says researchers had long debated the origins of the silver and that new geochemical evidence reconciles two competing theories. The blank describes the field before this firm evidence settled the issue.

steps.title: Reject wrong descriptions
steps.content: Choice A means known only to specialists, not unsettled. Choice B means well established, the opposite of a debated area. Choice C means alone or isolated, which does not fit competing theories. The word for a field based on conjecture before firm evidence arrived fits the context. The answer is D.
